settings.title: Settings

inputTapeUnderflow.legend: What should happen when the machine reads an empty cell from the input tape?
inputTapeUnderflow.random: Return a random integer from 0 to 9999

uninitializedRegisterRead.legend: What should happen when the machine reads from an uninitialized register?
uninitializedRegisterRead.random: Return a new random integer from 0 to 9999
uninitializedRegisterRead.superpositionCollapse: Stabilize the register at a random value from 0 to 9999

programCounterOutOfBounds.legend: What should happen when the program counter exits program bounds?
programCounterOutOfBounds.actAsHalt: Stop the machine normally (implicit HALT instruction)

ADDITION.title: Simple addition
description:
This is an example program
; that sums two numbers together.

You can compile it using the button below,
; and then use the "Run" button at the top of the page
; to execute the program.
comments: Load the first value from the input tape to r₀
Load the second value from the input tape to r₁
Add the value from r₁ to r₀, and store the result to r₀
Write the contents of r₀ to the output tape
End program execution

ABSOLUTE_VALUE.title: Absolute value
ABSOLUTE_VALUE.description: Calculates the absolute value of a given number.
comments: Read the value into r₀
If the number is greater than 0, skip to the end
Store the read value into r₁
Subtract r₁ from 0
Write r₀ to the output tape

N_TO_THE_N.title: nⁿ
N_TO_THE_N.description: A program that calculates n to the power of n.
comments: Read n from tape.
If n ≤ 0, write out 0 and end program execution.
If n > 0, execute the following algorithm:
Set the result variable contained in r₂ to n
Set the loop counter contained in r₃ to n - 1
Loop condition: r₃ > 0
Write the result to tape

FACTORIAL.title: n!
comments: Read n from tape, store into r₁
Set the result variable (r₂) to the initial value (1)
Loop condition: r₁ != 0
Multiply the result by the current loop counter (r₂ := r₁ * r₂)
Decrement the loop counter

SUM.title: Sum values
SUM.description: A program that sums read values until a zero is encountered.
comments: Load the initial value into r₁
Read a value from the input tape into r₀
Jump to the end of the program if the value read from tape is zero
Add the read value to r₁ and store the result in r₁
Write the result to tape

REVERSE_ARRAY.title: Reverse array

registers.r3: pointer to last loaded element + 1 (starts at 4)

comments: Load initial array pointer
Check if length > 0
Read element into *3
Increment array pointer
Decrement remaining elements counter
Now, write all elements in reverse order
Check if pointer is greater than 4
Decrement array pointer
Write the pointed element to tape

STRESS_TEST.title: Stress test
STRESS_TEST.description: A tight loop that copies values from the input tape to the output tape.
